rulings.Case Title: FERDINAND WILLIAM BISHOP VS. ADVOCATE MINES LTD. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hrg & Appl For Brent W. Coon, Esq. To Appear As Counsel Pro Hac Vice
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 26, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Plaintiff¿s application for Brent W. Coon to appear as counsel pro hac vice is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BEULAH MAE SIGSBEY et al VS. A.O. SMITH CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 26, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendants SB Decking, Inc. and Carrier Corporation¿s motion to stay for forum non conveniens is denied. Based on plaintiffs¿ representation that many, if not most, of the remaining defendants have been sued for alleged exposures occurring in California and/or in connection with Mr. Sigsbey¿s service in the Navy and the lack of significant evidence that defendants would be seriously inconvenienced by this case remaining in California, the public and private factors do not favor granting the motion. Defendant Carrier Corp.¿s sanctions request is denied. Plaintiffs did not commit any sanctionable conduct. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID J. PALOMARES VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 26, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Fisher Scientific Company LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH MCGREW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of Court'S Order Denying Its Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 26, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Campbell Soup Company¿s motion for reconsideration of the order denying its motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to submit any new facts, circumstances or law warranting reconsideration pursuant to CCP 1008(a). Even if defendant¿s summary judgment motion was reconsidered, it would be denied. The declaration of Dr. Birkner is an insufficient basis to disregard the declaration of Robert Cantley regarding asbestos content of insulation and fireproofing materials. = (220/HEK)
